Let's talk →

About me

Designer who codes.
Developer who cares.

I'm Celestino Trosso, a designer and developer based in Berlin. I've spent the last eight years at the intersection of craft and engineering — making things that are both beautiful to look at and well-built underneath.

I believe the distinction between "designer" and "developer" is mostly artificial. The best products come from people who can hold both in mind at once: how something looks and how it works, what it communicates and how it's constructed.

Before going independent, I worked at Loom and N26. I grew up in Seoul, went to university in Berlin, and decided to stay. Outside of work I cook, run, and maintain an embarrassingly large reading list.

How I work

Craft over speed

I'd rather spend an extra day getting the details right than ship something I'm not proud of. That said, I believe in iterating in the open — early, imperfect, real.

Opinions, held lightly

I'll tell you what I think and why. But I change my mind when the evidence warrants it. Good collaboration requires real positions, not just validation.

Systems over solutions

I try to solve problems in ways that prevent the next ten problems from existing. A good design system is worth a hundred one-off solutions.

Writing as thinking

If I can't explain a design decision in a sentence or two, I don't understand it well enough yet. Writing forces the precision that sketches let you avoid.
